title: portulaca pilosa
intro: Portulaca pilosa, also known as the purslane, is an annual succulent plant native to Asia and Africa, characterized by its small, pink or white flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestion. Herbal preparations of Portulaca pilosa include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, often used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
